A Typical Conversation: A bit of dialogue or slogans representative of the way society members think. Secret Society Descriptions 
Hl Anti-Mutant 
Beliefs: Eliminate mutant powers. Reestablish ppure-strain humans as the dominant force in 
society. There is an evil, monolithic conspiracy (of mutants to destroy true humans and impose ‘@ mutant dictatorship. If mutants are not destroyed, three-armed. green-tendrilled ‘monstrosities will take over! With their fantastic mental powers, they may be warping. your mind and playing with your emotions even ‘now! Kill any mutant, ary where, any time. Just because a mutant has registered with The ‘Computer is no reason to spare his life. Any deviation from the human norm is suspect. ‘Anyone who even looks at you cross-eyed is probably trying to control your mind. Mutants are evill Mutants are foul! Kill all mutants! Friends: Humanist. Enemies: Psion. General Descri he obvious irony of belonging to the Anti-Mutant secret society is, that all its player-character members are ‘mutants themselves, Unless the PC is ignorant of his own powers thmm,,.an interesting idea), being a member of Anti-Mutant is the equivalent of passing for white as a member of the Ku Klux Klan.
